title: De Wuchemärt
desc: The weekly market on the Reuss

sn.s: Am Zischtig isch Wuchemärt a de Reuss.
sn.e: On Tuesday there's a weekly market on the Reuss.
The farmers sell vegetables and fruit.

sn.s: Alls isch frisch und regional, vo da.
sn.e: Everything is fresh and regional, from here.

sn.s: "Grüezi, was wänd Sie?" fragt e Buurin.
sn.e: "Hello, what would you like?" a farmer's wife asks.
"How much are the apples?" I ask.
"May I try one?" I ask politely.

sn.s: "Klar, nämed Sie!" Sie git mer es Stückli.
sn.e: "Of course, take one!" She gives me a little piece.

sn.s: De Öpfel isch süess und saftig.
sn.e: The apple is sweet and juicy.

sn.s: "Sehr guet. Ich nimm es Kilo, bitte."
sn.e: "Very good. I'll take a kilo, please."

sn.s: Sie packt d'Öpfel i e Papiersack.
sn.e: She packs the apples into a paper bag.

sn.s: Am Märt z'choufe macht würkli Freud.
sn.e: Shopping at the market is really a joy.

sn.s: D'Lüt sind fründlich und nämed sich Ziit.
sn.e: The people are friendly and take their time.

sn.s: So lärn ich Luzärn vo siner beschte Siite kenne.
sn.e: This way I get to know Lucerne from its best side.
